META PLATFORMS ACQUIRES HUMANOID ROBOT STARTUP ASSURED ROBOT INTELLIGENCE
articles.summary: Meta has acquired a startup that is trying to build humanoid robots. Assured Robot Intelligence is a company at the frontier of robotic intelligence. Its team will join Meta to focus on optimizing models for robotics applications. Investors are skeptical of Meta's spending plans - the company's stock was down following the announcement of the acquisition.

articles.title: SPACEX HAS SPENT MORE THAN $15 BILLION ON STARSHIP AND IS RACING TO MAKE ROCKETRY RESEMBLE AN AIRLINE SCHEDULE
articles.summary: SpaceX has so far spent more than $15 billion developing Starship. The company's capital expenditure surged nearly fivefold between 2024 and 2025 to $20.7 billion. Of that spend, $12.7 billion was directed at AI initiatives. Starlink is still the company's financial engine, making $11.4 billion in revenue in 2025.

articles.title: THE COMPANY THAT BUILT TIKTOK'S ALGORITHM IS NOW DESIGNING DRUGS FOR DISEASES PHARMA CALLED UNDRUGGABLE
articles.summary: ByteDance has entered the AI drug discovery race. The company's drug discovery unit, Anew Labs, recently presented its first AI-designed therapy at a major immunology conference. Its work targeted a protein-protein interaction long thought to be undruggable. The unit has published a generative model that it claims can design functional molecules across all scales.

articles.title: AMERICA'S ELECTRICITY GAP
articles.summary: Power access is incredibly important to tech companies' gamble on AI development. The US was always going to need more power investment to meet its next phase of economic growth and was already struggling to meet rising electricity demand well before the AI boom. The costs of the electricity gap are mounting as power becomes more important to the national economy. More of the US will have to permit the fast deployment of solar and batteries if they want to meet their own increasing electricity needs.

AGENT SKILLS
articles.summary: AI coding agents are extremely capable, but they have no instinct for the parts of the job that don't show up in the diff. Agents will skip the senior-engineering work unless you make it impossible to skip. The job increasingly is to encode discipline as something agents can't talk themselves out of. Skills are one way to do that. The senior-engineer parts of the job are no longer optional, even when the engineer is a model.

articles.title: OPEN DESIGN
articles.summary: Open Design is an open-source alternative to Claude Design. It uses the same loop and artifact-first mental model with none of the lock-in. Open Design is local-first, web deployable, and supports several coding agents. It behaves like a senior designer with a working filesystem, a deterministic palette library, and a checklist culture.

articles.title: SOMETIMES SMART PLANNERS LOSE TO SIMPLE MARKETS
OPENAI WANTS TO GO PUBLIC. FIRST SARAH FRIAR NEEDS TO GET IT TO GROW UP
articles.summary: Sarah Friar was brought into OpenAI to give it the financial maturity needed to go public. Over the past few months, she has balanced the company's spending expectations and questioned the wisdom of spending even more money on data centers. It is up to Friar to pull off what could be one of the biggest IPOs ever. If she moves too slowly, OpenAI could fall behind and hit the market after Anthropic. Moving quickly risks stretching the business too far.
